name: Kinome-ae (Bamboo Shoots with Green Soy Dress
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the bamboo shoots: Place them on one side, cut off the base, and cut it in half horizontally. Cut the top lengthways into quarters. Cut all pieces into 1/2-inch dice.
2. Boil water for the bamboo shoots until boiling, then drop in the shoots and return to boil. Cook uncovered for 10 minutes until tender. Drain and set aside.
3. In a medium saucepan, bring 3/8 pint of dashi, sugar, 1/4 tsp salt, MSG, and kombu to a boil over high heat. Add the bamboo shoots and continue boiling until almost all the liquid is gone. Remove from heat and let cool.
4. Wash spinach leaves thoroughly and pat dry. Grind the leaves into a paste, adding the remaining salt slowly. Mix with 3/8 pint of water in a small saucepan. Bring to boil and pour through a fine sieve, discarding the liquid.
5. To serve: Pour the miso dressing into a bowl and rub the spinach paste through a sieve into the dressing. Stir until the mixture is soft and delicate green. Sprinkle with 1/4 tsp of Kona Sansho powder. Add the bamboo shoots and gently stir together. Serve at room temperature.
